data.narrative:
QuantWare is a Series B quantum computing company founded by Matt Rijlaarsdam and Alessandro Bruno, having raised $178M to date. The quantum computing sector comprises 11 comparable companies—3 public (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Systems) and 8 private peers including Q-CTRL, Quantinuum, and Rigetti Computing.

The valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method due to constraints in peer comparability. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a baseline proxy, applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B progression to the next financing stage, yielding an implied valuation of $338M. The peer-median method is not applicable; while 7 same-sector peers exist, insufficient overlap across same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier filters prevented reliable median extraction without risking sector-wide distortion.

The triangulated range supports a valuation of $287M to $389M, with $338M as the central estimate. Key uncertainties stem from reliance on a single method and the heterogeneity of the quantum computing landscape, where companies pursue divergent technology pathways (superconducting, trapped-ion, photonic) and business models, limiting direct comparability. Refinement would benefit from additional same-stage private transactions or public comparable metrics indexed to quantum computing–specific KPIs.
